review_text: My favorite movie of all time. My grandma and my cousin and I went to see the first movie in theaters when I was 11, and I lost my tooth that night. Still a fond memory of mine. The live action version cannot compare to this beautiful work of art.

How to Train Your Dragon is an interesting, adventurous story about how a young viking forms a bond with a dragon. I’m fond of the soundtrack in the movie because it gives a heroic feeling. Although the story is a bit predictable what you can expect from a feel-good animation movie. There are heartwarming, humorous and unexpected twists to the plot. Overall, How to Train Your Dragon is an exiting movie and while I don’t particularly like animated movies, this one is an all time classic.

One of the best dreamworks movies of all time! The story telling and atmosphere is absolutely breathtaking; although one of a more rushed movie. I wish they would have had more scenes building the other dragon riders, but still a fantastic movie to watch with anyone!

It has arguably been a long time since I watched this movie. I have very fond memories of it, and it did what it did really well. I watched some recaps for this review. The animation is stunning, and the story here is better than most Dreamworks stories out there. Hiccup is a great main protagonist as he challenges the views of his culture, and works towards helping them grow past their biases and misconceptions. I am excited to revisit this film with the new live action version.
This movie is just pure Awesome!
